title: Set 1: General Knowledge
description: 50 questions covering Nepal geography, history, politics, international affairs, and science.

questions.question: What is the total area of Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Nepal covers an area of 1,47,181 square kilometers (56,827 sq mi), stretching approximately 885 km east-west and 145-241 km north-south.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Karnali River is the longest river in Nepal, stretching approximately 1,080 km in total length.

questions.question: What is the highest peak in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Mount Everest (Sagarmatha) at 8,848.86 meters is the highest peak in Nepal and the world.

questions.question: Which is the largest lake in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Rara Lake in Mugu district is the largest lake in Nepal, covering about 10.8 sq km at an altitude of 2,990 meters.

questions.question: Which national park in Nepal is a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Both Chitwan National Park (natural heritage, 1984) and Sagarmatha National Park (natural heritage, 1979) are UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
questions.reference: UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Nepal

questions.question: What is the lowest point in Nepal (in terms of elevation)?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Kechana Kalan in Jhapa district is the lowest point in Nepal at approximately 59 meters above sea level.

questions.question: How many districts are there in Nepal currently?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Nepal currently has 77 districts, after the addition of Nawalparasi East, Nawalparasi West, Rukum East, and Rukum West during federal restructuring.

questions.question: Which is the deepest gorge in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Kali Gandaki Gorge (Andha Galchi) is considered one of the deepest gorges in the world, between Annapurna and Dhaulagiri massifs.

questions.question: Which region in Nepal receives the highest rainfall?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Panchthar district in Province No. 1 receives the highest rainfall in Nepal, with some areas getting over 5,000 mm annually.

questions.question: What is the largest national park in Nepal by area?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Shey Phoksundo National Park in Dolpa district is the largest national park in Nepal, covering 3,555 sq km.

questions.question: When was the unification of Nepal completed under Prithvi Narayan Shah?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Prithvi Narayan Shah completed the unification of Nepal in 1769 (1826 BS) after capturing Kathmandu Valley.

questions.question: When was the Sugauli Treaty signed between Nepal and British India?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Sugauli Treaty was signed on March 4, 1816, after the Anglo-Nepalese War (1814-1816).

questions.question: When was the People's Movement (Jana Andolan I) that restored multi-party democracy?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The People's Movement (Jana Andolan I) of 1990 (2046 BS) ended the Panchayat system and restored multi-party democracy.

questions.question: When was the Comprehensive Peace Agreement (CPA) signed in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Comprehensive Peace Agreement was signed on November 21, 2006 (Mangsir 5, 2063 BS) between the government and Maoists.

questions.question: When was the Republic of Nepal declared?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Federal Democratic Republic of Nepal was declared on May 28, 2008 (Jestha 15, 2065 BS) by the first Constituent Assembly.

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Father of the Nation' in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Prithvi Narayan Shah, who unified Nepal, is regarded as the 'Father of the Nation' (Rashtriya Pita).

questions.question: When did Nepal become a member of the United Nations?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Nepal became a member of the United Nations on December 14, 1955.

questions.question: Which treaty opened Nepal to foreign trade in 1923?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Nepal-Britain Treaty of 1923 recognized Nepal as an independent and sovereign nation and opened trade.

questions.question: When was the first election held in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The first general election in Nepal was held in 1959 (2015 BS), in which the Nepali Congress won a majority.

questions.question: When was the Kot Massacre (Kot Parva) that established Rana rule?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Kot Massacre occurred on September 14, 1846, after which Jung Bahadur Rana established the Rana oligarchy.

questions.question: How many provinces were established under the new Constitution of Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Constitution of Nepal 2015 established seven provinces for federal governance.

questions.question: What is the name of Province No. 1 after it was renamed?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Province No. 1 was officially renamed as Koshi Province (Koshi Pradesh) in 2023.

questions.question: What is the name of Province No. 5 after it was renamed?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Province No. 5 was officially renamed as Lumbini Province (Lumbini Pradesh) in 2020.

questions.question: Which is the capital city of Gandaki Province?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Pokhara is the capital city of Gandaki Province, designated in 2020.

questions.question: Which is the capital city of Sudurpashchim Province?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Dhangadhi is the capital city of Sudurpashchim Province, designated in 2019.

questions.question: When was the last census conducted in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The last National Population and Housing Census was conducted in 2078 BS (2021 AD), recording approximately 29.19 million population.

questions.question: What is the population of Nepal according to the 2021 census?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: According to the 2021 census, Nepal's population is approximately 29.19 million.

questions.question: Which is the most populous district in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Kathmandu district is the most populous district in Nepal with over 2 million people according to the 2021 census.

questions.question: Which is the least populous district in Nepal?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Manang district is the least populous district in Nepal with approximately 5,600 people.

questions.question: What is the literacy rate of Nepal according to the 2021 census?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The literacy rate of Nepal is approximately 76.3% according to the 2021 census.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of SAARC located?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The headquarters of SAARC (South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation) is located in Kathmandu, Nepal.

questions.question: How many member states are there in SAARC?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: SAARC has eight member states: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in New York City, USA.

questions.question: How many permanent members are there in the UN Security Council?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The UN Security Council has five permanent members: USA, UK, France, Russia, and China.

questions.question: Which country is NOT a member of BIMSTEC?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: BIMSTEC members are Bangladesh, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Bhutan, and Nepal. China is not a member.

questions.question: When was the United Nations established?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The United Nations was established on October 24, 1945, after World War II.

questions.question: Which is the largest continent in the world by area?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent, covering approximately 44.58 million square kilometers.

questions.question: Which is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: Vatican City (Holy See) is the smallest country in the world, covering only 0.44 sq km.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in the world?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Nile River is traditionally considered the longest river at approximately 6,650 km, though the Amazon is close.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest by area?
questions.subject: GK
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean, covering about 165.25 million square kilometers.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula of water?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, with the chemical formula H2O.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet due to iron oxide (rust) on its surface, which gives it a reddish appearance.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in vacuum?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792 km/s, commonly rounded to 3,00,000 km/s.

questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere, followed by oxygen at about 21%.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of electric current?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: The SI unit of electric current is the Ampere (A), named after André-Marie Ampère.

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing the telephone in 1876.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: Diamond is the hardest known natural material, rated 10 on the Mohs scale of mineral hardness.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced when the skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin when exposed to ultraviolet B (UVB) radiation from sunlight.

questions.question: What is the largest organ in the human body?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ of the human body, with a total area of about 20 square feet in adults.

questions.question: Which blood group is known as the universal donor?
questions.subject: SCIENCE
questions.explanation: O negative (O-) is the universal donor because it lacks A, B, and Rh(D) antigens, making it compatible with all blood types.
